feed.title: HA News Feed — Pokémon GO, Brawl Stars & AI
feed.subtitle: Aggregated news: Pokémon GO, Brawl Stars & AI

🤖 Samsung will soon start charging to access its smart home API
entry.summary: From October this year Samsung will roll out a variety of new paid tiers for access to its SmartThings API, including a $4.99 monthly plan for "non-commercial, individual developers." It won't just be developers that pay the price though. Some more advanced smart home users are likely to fall afoul

entry.title: 🤖 Apple’s most powerful Macs might be waiting until 2027 for big processor upgrades
entry.summary: Apple is expected to shake up its usual Mac silicon release strategy, with Bloomberg's Mark Gurman reporting that there won't be Pro or Max versions of the upcoming M6 chip. Instead, Apple wants to "fast-track technologies that it originally planned to release later" with the M7 launch next year. Th

entry.title: 🤖 It’s the last day of Prime Day — here are over 130 great deals to choose from
entry.summary: We’ve arrived at the final day of Prime Day, which at this point should probably be called “Prime Week.” We’ve found discounts on all manner of gadgets, including TVs, smart home tech, chargers, headphones, and more. Some of the best deals have started selling out at some retailers, so if you’ve bee

entry.title: 🤖 Trump Mobile will take your $499 right now
entry.summary: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We still don't have the phones we preordered yet, but this week the T1 hit open sale, no deposit required. Trump Mobile's T1 Phone is now available for anyone to buy directly. The phone has previously trickled out to a small n

entry.title: 🤖 This year’s Prime Day deals on Apple products are the best I’ve seen
entry.summary: Amazon’s Prime Day is now in its third day, and whether you’re looking for a new pair of wireless earbuds or a smartwatch, there’s a good chance you’ll find a discount. The Apple Watch Series 11 has already dropped to a new low price, while the AirPods Pro 3 are discounted to $179. With Apple […]

entry.title: 🤖 OpenAI will delay GPT-5.6 after Trump administration request
entry.summary: The Trump administration, apprehensive of potential security issues, has reportedly asked OpenAI to stagger the release of its next big-ticket model, GPT-5.6. The Information reported that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told employees Wednesday in a company Q&A that it would release GPT-5.6 in limited previe

entry.title: 🤖 Framework has good news and bad news
entry.summary: Thanks to the component crisis, it's a bad time to want a new computer. But if you are waiting on a preorder for the Framework Laptop 13 Pro - which Framework's CEO has called the "MacBook Pro for Linux users" - the company shared good news on Thursday that might mean yours will cost less […]

entry.title: 🤖 MIT in the media: Exploring how curiosity-driven science is an essential ingredient in America’s success
entry.summary: “Scientific American” showcases the history and future of America’s scientific engine, highlighting promising young scientists and icons at MIT and beyond.

entry.title: 🤖 Improving the speed and energy-efficiency of AI agents
entry.summary: A new system, known as Murakkab, optimizes the design and deployment of multistep workflows that power AI applications.

entry.title: 🤖 How agents are transforming work
entry.summary: A new OpenAI research paper shows how AI agents are transforming work, enabling longer, more complex tasks and expanding productivity across roles.

entry.title: 🤖 OpenAI and Broadcom unveil LLM-optimized inference chip
entry.summary: OpenAI and Broadcom introduce Jalapeño, a custom AI chip built for LLM inference to improve performance, efficiency, and scale across AI systems.

entry.title: 🤖 Exploring the societal impacts of AI
entry.summary: During the AI and Society Forum, leading MIT researchers examined critical questions about AI’s influence on employment and democracy.

entry.title: 🤖 How GPT-5 helped immunologist Derya Unutmaz solve a 3-year-old mystery
entry.summary: GPT-5 Pro helped solve a 3-year-old immunology mystery, offering insights into T cell behavior. The breakthrough could support cancer and autoimmune research.

entry.title: 🤖 Helping build shared standards for advanced AI
entry.summary: OpenAI helps build shared standards for advanced AI, supporting evaluation frameworks, safety practices, and global cooperation through the Appia Foundation.

entry.title: 🤖 New chip could help tiny robots traverse complex environments
entry.summary: Researchers combined an efficient algorithm with dedicated hardware to rapidly generate 3D maps for navigation using minimal memory and power.

entry.title: 🤖 How Omio is building the future of conversational travel
entry.summary: Discover how Omio uses OpenAI to power conversational travel experiences, accelerate product development, and transform into an AI-native company.

entry.title: 🤖 A better way to model the behavior of metal alloys
entry.summary: MIT researchers’ approach captures subtle atomic patterns, improving predictions of material properties.

entry.title: 🤖 MIT in the media: For the future of tech, "Massachusetts can absolutely lead"
entry.summary: Leaders, faculty across MIT discuss fostering innovation and talent in Greater Boston in special series of articles published alongside the outlet's annual list of 'Tech Power Players'

entry.title: 🏆 THE ADIDAS STARR CUP IS ON!
🤖 In game theory, generalists sometimes win out over specialists
entry.summary: Researchers show that for certain kinds of games, an overlooked class of algorithms performs much better than expected.

entry.title: 🤖 Could AI tell you where you left your keys?
entry.summary: A new spatial memory system for robots efficiently captures details about the objects they see while exploring their environment.

entry.title: 🏆 Brawl Stars Challengers Finals: Tickets Available Now!
🏆 THE FORMULA BRAWL EVENT IS HERE!
🤖 Google just redesigned the search box for the first time in 25 years — here’s why it matters more than you think.
entry.summary: For a quarter century, the Google search box has been one of the most recognizable interfaces in computing: a thin white rectangle, a blinking cursor, a few typed words, and a list of blue links. On Tuesday, Google will formally retire that paradigm. At its annual I/O developer conference , Google a

entry.title: 🏆 Brawl Cup Event Guide
🏆 Release Notes April 2026
🏆 Changes to Bling, Shop, and Cosmetics!
🤖 Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
entry.summary: Railway , a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B funding round, as surging demand for artificial intelligence applications exposes the limitations of leg

entry.title: 🤖 Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
entry.summary: The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive. Claude Code , Anthropic's terminal-based AI agent that can write, debug, and deploy code autonomously, has captured the imagination of software developers worldwide. But its pricing — ranging from $20 to $200 per month

entry.title: 🤖 Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
entry.summary: Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options. His startup, Listen Labs , needed to hire over 100 engineers, but competing against Mark Zuckerberg's $100 million offers seemed impossible. So he spent $5,000 — a fifth of his marketing budget — on a billboard in San Francisco displaying what looked like

entry.title: 🤖 Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
entry.summary: Salesforce on Tuesday launched an entirely rebuilt version of Slackbot , the company's workplace assistant, transforming it from a simple notification tool into what executives describe as a fully powered AI agent capable of searching enterprise data, drafting documents, and taking action on behalf

entry.title: 🤖 Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
entry.summary: Anthropic released Cowork on Monday, a new AI agent capability that extends the power of its wildly successful Claude Code tool to non-technical users — and according to company insiders, the team built the entire feature in approximately a week and a half, largely using Claude Code itself. The laun

entry.title: 🤖 Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment
entry.summary: Nous Research , the open-source artificial intelligence startup backed by crypto venture firm Paradigm , released a new competitive programming model on Monday that it says matches or exceeds several larger proprietary systems — trained in just four days using 48 of Nvidia's latest B200 graphics pro
